title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description:
Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth.

The maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root node down to the farthest leaf node.
hint: Use recursion or breadth-first search to count levels in the tree.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description:
Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree by swapping every left child with its right child.

Example:
Input:
    4
   / \
  2   7

Output:
    4
   / \
  7   2
hint: Visit each node and swap its left and right children.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
description:
Given the root of a binary tree, determine if it is a valid binary search tree.

Example:
Input:
[2,1,3]
Output:
true
hint: Each node must fall within a valid range of values.

title: Lowest Common Ancestor of Binary Tree
description:
Given a binary tree and two nodes, find their lowest common ancestor.

Example:
Input:
root=[3,5,1,6,2,0,8], p=5, q=1
Output:
3
hint: Use recursion to search both left and right subtrees.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description:
Design an algorithm to serialize a binary tree into a string and deserialize the string back into the original tree.

Example:
Input:
[1,2,3,null,null,4,5]

Output:
The same binary tree structure
hint: Use DFS traversal and store node values with markers for null nodes.

title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
description:
Given a binary tree, find the maximum path sum. A path can start and end at any node in the tree.

Example:
Input:
[1,2,3]

Output:
6
hint: Use recursion to calculate the maximum contribution from each node.
